hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Look at the problem name. What can be premiered at a wrong time?

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: Yes, the video editorial. Where can you usually find an editorial?

hints.title: Hint 3
hints.content: Yes, the blog. In the last paragraphs of the blog, there's a link to the editorial. Click it and watch it carefully.

hints.title: Hint 4
hints.content: Is there anything unusual with this editorial?

hints.title: Hint 5
hints.content: You're right! Who puts musics while recording a video editorial? Also, Kent mentioned that "the songs we're using as the background music are in our favorite playlist; we enjoy them a lot, and you should definitely check them out!" Figure out what the songs are!

hints.title: Hint 6
hints.content: There's still something accessible. Can you find it?

hints.title: Hint 7
hints.content: Brilliant idea to steal the solution from the slide! The link is clearly visible in the video. You can manually copy the URL and read it at your end.

solutions.title: Solution
solutions.content:
In Step 4, you can use Shazam, Google's hum-a-song search, or any other music identification tools, and find that the musics (in playing order) are:
What's The Problem? (by OSKI)
23 (by Diamond Eyes)
Time (by OVSKY)
51 (by THYKIER)
A&B (by MADZI & ETikka)
On (by Alisky)
Code (by MANSHN)
Forces (by Jim Yosef)
Combining all these words, you'll get "what's the problem 23 time 51 A&B on code forces". As $$$23 \times 51 = 1173$$$, this is asking what 1173A and 1173B are on Codeforces.
If you read
the slide
, you can find the input and output format of this problem. The input can be either
first
or
second
. Therefore, if the input is
first
, output
Nauuo and Votes
; otherwise, output
Nauuo and Chess
.
